title: Auto-detect repo
body: Reads the git remote — no config required. Override with -repo owner/name for any other repo.

title: Idempotent
body: Re-run freely. An identity marker in the issue body prevents duplicates even if the ref cache is lost.

title: Tracker-agnostic
body: Not just GitHub. Project an OpenSpec change onto a Beads task graph with -provider beads — the WorkProvider interface keeps the tracker your choice.

title: Cross-repo linking
body: specsync link writes links.md and pushes a Related section to both issues automatically.

title: Issue-first pull
body: Pull any existing issue into a local spec. The ref is cached so subsequent pushes update — never duplicate.

title: Planning scan
body: specsync scan shows what already exists for an area — in-flight changes, loose issues, recent commits — before you plan.

title: Release follow-up
body: specsync release-plan reconstructs what shipped, what's loose, and an advisory bump — using OpenSpec requirement deltas as a release signal no commit-only tool can see.

title: Agent-friendly
body: A bundled skill installs for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Copilot, or any agentskills.io-compatible .agents directory.

title: Preview writes
body: sync, pull, and link support -dry-run. Read-only inspection commands need no preview mode.

title: Auto-lifecycle
body: stage:active while work remains; auto-flips to stage:complete when all tasks are checked. Optional -close-completed keeps the issue open/closed state aligned — completion closes it, new work reopens it.

title: Two-way reconcile
body: Checkboxes ticked in the GitHub issue merge back into your local tasks.md on the next sync — progress flows both ways, never lost in either direction.

title: Traceability
body: specsync trace reconstructs how a change, its issue, PRs, and commits connect — every link backed by evidence (markers, branches, commit footers), never guessed.

title: Projects boards
body: Point -project owner/number at a GitHub Projects board: synced issues land on the board, Status follows the change lifecycle, and human curation is respected.

title: Spec-driven changelog
body: specsync changelog writes a Keep a Changelog section from your shipped changes — one entry per change, release notes authored at planning time, never a raw commit dump.
